summary: The papers focus on advanced sensing, tracking, and security for small UAS. One study develops a 3D LiDAR-based tracking system using adaptive Extended Kalman Filtering to improve relative positioning for swarm flight under varying lighting. Another explores visual cooperative tracking combined with open-path gas sensors for environmental monitoring, leveraging Tunable Diode Laser Absorption Spectroscopy. A third presents a multi-physics simulation framework for designing high-power microwave counter-UAS systems, addressing non-kinetic neutralization of autonomous drones. A fourth introduces a lightweight authenticated encryption framework tailored for IoT devices like drones, ensuring secure telemetry under tight resource constraints. The final paper proposes a modified YOLO-based architecture optimized for real-time detection of small, fast-moving UAVs in challenging visual conditions.
